outcome_text: Privacy risk of the AI system—as identified in the MAP function—is examined and documented.
scoring_text: Skill in AI privacy risk assessment, including examining privacy risks throughout the AI lifecycle, evaluating data protection adequacy, assessing re-identification and inference risks, and documenting privacy risk findings against MAP-phase identified risks.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in AI privacy risk assessment, including examining privacy risks throughout the AI lifecycle, evaluating data protection adequacy, assessing re-identification and inference risks, and documenting privacy risk findings against MAP-phase identified risks.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI risk categories, including performance risk, bias and fairness risk, explainability risk, robustness risk, safety risk, privacy risk, and regulatory compliance risk.
comments: NIST AI RMF MS-2.10 (MEASURE) superset of WIDAI AG domain. AG-K-010 is the strongest match (STS: 0.588, cosine: 0.674). Secondary: SP-S-001 (SP, cosine: 0.671), SP-A-002 (SP, cosine: 0.670).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.9/10 (raw 0.588). Shared vocabulary: [ai, privacy, risk]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [adequacy, against, assessing, assessment, data, documenting] vs WIDAI terms [bias, categories, compliance, explainability, fairness, knowledge] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.884 (P=0.875, R=0.894). Balanced P/R — the 32-word competency implication and 24-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (98.9%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the NIST AI RMF element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.674 > STS 0.588 (gap +0.086). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, privacy, risk] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 13.2%. Shared tokens: [ai, privacy, risk]. NIST AI RMF-only: [adequacy, against, assessing, assessment, data, documenting]. WIDAI-only: [bias, categories, compliance, explainability, fairness, knowledge].
